text: The way light passes through a glass of water sitting on a windowsill. There is something in how it bends and scatters, throwing small trembling shapes onto the table or the wall nearby, shapes that have no name and last only as long as the sun holds its angle. The glass itself is ordinary, the water is ordinary, and yet together they do something the eye keeps returning to without quite being able to explain why. It feels accidental, like the world briefly forgot to be plain.
